name: ExtensionAge_Comparator
title: R5: < | <= | >= | > | ad - how to understand the value
purpose:
This extension is part of the cross-version definitions generated to enable use of the
element `Age.comparator` as defined in FHIR R5
in FHIR R4.

The source element is defined as:
`Age.comparator` 0..1 `code`

Across FHIR versions, the element set has been mapped as:
*  R5: `Age.comparator` 0..1 `code`
*  R4B: `Age.comparator` 0..1 `code`
*  R4: `Age.comparator` 0..1 `code`

Following are the generation technical comments:
Element `Age.comparator` is mapped to FHIR R4 element `Age.comparator` as `SourceIsBroaderThanTarget`.
The target context `Age.comparator` is a modifier element, so this extension does not need to be defined as a modifier.

element.short: R5: < | <= | >= | > | ad - how to understand the value
element.comment:
Element `Age.comparator` is mapped to FHIR R4 element `Age.comparator` as `SourceIsBroaderThanTarget`.
The target context `Age.comparator` is a modifier element, so this extension does not need to be defined as a modifier.

element.short: < | <= | >= | > | ad - how to understand the value
element.definition: How the value should be understood and represented - whether the actual value is greater or less than the stated value due to measurement issues; e.g. if the comparator is "<" , then the real value is < stated value.

binding.description: How the Quantity should be understood and represented.
